title: EU approves a $106 billion loan package to help Ukraine after Hungary lifts its veto
description: The European Union on Thursday approved a $106-billion loan package to help Ukraine meet its economic and military needs for two years, ending months of political deadlock.

title: U.S. soldier accused of pocketing $400,000 through bets on Maduro’s capture
description: The bet on the seizing of the Venezuelan leader has drawn scrutiny to insider trading within the growing prediction market industry.

title: Trump intends to invite Putin to G-20 summit in Miami, officials say
description: The Russian leader has not yet committed to attend the annual gathering of world leaders, which is scheduled for December at the president’s Doral golf resort.

title: Third U.S. aircraft carrier arrives in waters near Iran
description: The path of the USS George H.W. Bush to the Middle East has been closely watched as President Trump demands progress in peace negotiations with Tehran.

title: DOJ watchdog launches review of agency’s compliance with Epstein files law
description: The audit by the department’s inspector general follows months of lawmaker complaints over the congressionally mandated release of investigative files.

title: New Republican plan would fund ICE for rest of Trump’s term
description: Republicans took a step without the help of Democrats toward fully reopening the Department of Homeland Security and ending the partial government shutdown.

title: Trump unveils new drug price deal with Regeneron amid affordability push
description: The drug company is the 17th to agree to lower its prices as part of the administration’s Most Favored Nation initiative. It will make a new gene therapy available free.

title: Here are Virginia’s new congressional districts and who is running
description: Some House races are newly competitive, and four Republican incumbents are now endangered.

title: Trump officials reclassify medical marijuana as lower-risk drug
description: The Justice Department said the drug will now be listed on Schedule III, similar to some common prescription painkillers. The order does not decriminalize marijuana for recreational use.

title: Who's Trump listening to on Iran?
description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with White House Correspondent Franco Ordonez and National Security Correspondent Greg Myre about how President Trump's Cabinet is shaping the process of the Iran war.

title: ‘FBoy Island’ contestant’s bet on his own Senate bid exposes perils of Kalshi
description: A former reality TV contestant turned candidate in Virginia and two U.S. House hopefuls in Texas and Minnesota have been banned from the prediction market for five years.

title: John Phelan forced out as Navy secretary after 13 months
description: The billionaire was a significant contributor to President Donald Trump’s campaign, but officials said he clashed repeatedly with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

title: Can a mentalist trick Trump? Oz Pearlman will try in a room full of journalists
description: The White House Correspondents' Dinner will be headlined by a mentalist instead of a comedian. Oz Pearlman tells NPR he hopes to unify, delight and puzzle the crowd — but can't reveal how.

title: Who’s out in the Trump administration’s spring shakeup
description: At least five officials have left Trump’s Cabinet and U.S. military leadership since the start of the war with Iran. Here’s a look at who left and why.

title: Congress is supposed to police its own ethics. Here’s why it falls short.
description: A spate of recent ethics cases illustrates the limits of a process that is slow and prone to political calculation.

title: Trump announces a 3-week extension of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
description: President Trump said Thursday that Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks after the two sides met at the White House for a high-level negotiation.

title: 4 charts show where money is going in the midterms -- and who has the most cash
description: The latest campaign finance reports show Democratic enthusiasm in key House and Senate races, but national Republican groups have far more in the bank to potentially spend down the road.

title: Senate votes to kickstart partisan funding process for ICE. Here's how that works
description: A budget plan with a $70 billion expected price tag for immigration enforcement advanced after an overnight session. It now heads to the House of Representatives.

title: Trump facing an uphill battle with voters as polling numbers continue to tumble
description: President Trump's polling is nearing an all-time low as he remains mired in a war and political fights that haven't turned out as he's expected.

title: President Trump's popularity is falling. What does it mean for him and the GOP?
description: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Jonah Goldberg, editor-in-chief of The Dispatch, about President Trump's declining public approval.

title: RFK Jr. faces questions on vaccines, measles and more at Senate hearing
description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. appeared before the Senate Finance Committee and the Senate HELP Committee Wednesday to answer questions about measles, vaccines, nutrition and budget cuts.

title: Trump team defends redistricting push as GOP faces limited gains
description: After a Virginia vote pushes seats toward Democrats, Republicans turn to Florida and the courts to claw back an edge.

title: Former congressman Devin Nunes departs as CEO of Trump media company
description: The onetime lawmaker is leaving Trump Media & Technology, which operates the social media platform Truth Social.

title: Democratic Rep. David Scott of Georgia dies at 80
description: The lawmaker, who had served in the chamber since 2003, was seeking a 13th term in his safely Democratic district.

title: Pentagon says Navy secretary is leaving, the latest departure of a top defense leader
description: Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell said John Phelan, the Navy's top civilian official, was "departing the administration, effective immediately." Navy Undersecretary Hung Cao will become acting secretary of the Navy.

title: Clearing Strait of Hormuz of mines could take 6 months, Pentagon tells Congress
description: The Pentagon assessment, shared in a classified briefing for lawmakers, suggests gasoline and oil prices could remain elevated through the midterm elections.

title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington Post and the Partnership for Public Service.

title: Supreme Court allows soldier to sue contractor over suicide bombing
description: The ruling will make it easier for troops hurt in combat zones to win damages as contractors become a larger part of military operations around the world.

title: Mapping where the redistricting fight stands and where it’s headed
description: As Trump pushes for a more Republican-friendly House map, more than half a dozen states are potential targets for mid-decade tweaks to congressional boundaries.

title: Six takeaways from Virginia’s vote to boost Democrats in national redistricting war
description: The measure puts Democrats ahead in the battle for the House, but attention now shifts to Florida, where Republicans could regain the gerrymandering advantage.

title: Trump eyes Iran deal with many of the trade-offs he blasted Obama for accepting
description: As U.S. envoys pursue peace talks, the president confronts persistent sticking points: sanctions relief, enrichment limits and the risk of empowering Tehran.
